Coastal Emergency scenario:
Storm season is coming. You need to wire a resilient emergency communication network across coastal villages.

You may choose any subset of the available cable links, but the chosen cable network must be a single connected tree that includes every required terminal village.
Every village that lies in your chosen network is active and must be assigned a radio frequency.
If an interference pair is active on both ends, those two villages must use different frequencies.

sub_instance.problem_statement:
You run a two-factory manufacturing pipeline. Factory A makes components, then Factory B finishes the same jobs.
Each factory is its own job shop with one-job-per-machine exclusivity and no preemption.
Every job must finish all of its Factory A operations before that same job may start any Factory B operation.
Your objective is to minimize the coupled makespan: the time when the final Factory B operation finishes.

Instance size: 6 jobs, 7 machines in each factory.
Jobs and ordered operations:
- Job 1: Factory A MA4 (2) -> MA1 (4) -> MA2 (9) -> MA3 (6) -> MA7 (3) -> MA5 (9) -> MA6 (6); Factory B MB7 (9) -> MB3 (8) -> MB1 (9) -> MB6 (2) -> MB4 (2) -> MB2 (6) -> MB5 (7)
- Job 2: Factory A MA3 (2) -> MA2 (4) -> MA6 (7) -> MA4 (9) -> MA1 (8) -> MA7 (7) -> MA5 (8); Factory B MB5 (9) -> MB1 (8) -> MB7 (8) -> MB2 (1) -> MB3 (8) -> MB6 (5) -> MB4 (3)
- Job 3: Factory A MA1 (2) -> MA4 (4) -> MA6 (3) -> MA5 (3) -> MA7 (4) -> MA2 (3) -> MA3 (6); Factory B MB4 (4) -> MB5 (3) -> MB6 (9) -> MB3 (9) -> MB1 (8) -> MB7 (6) -> MB2 (5)
- Job 4: Factory A MA3 (1) -> MA4 (4) -> MA6 (1) -> MA5 (3) -> MA2 (5) -> MA7 (9) -> MA1 (2); Factory B MB4 (5) -> MB3 (1) -> MB5 (1) -> MB1 (9) -> MB2 (3) -> MB6 (1) -> MB7 (5)
- Job 5: Factory A MA2 (9) -> MA5 (3) -> MA3 (2) -> MA1 (2) -> MA4 (1) -> MA7 (6) -> MA6 (3); Factory B MB5 (9) -> MB3 (4) -> MB2 (5) -> MB6 (2) -> MB1 (2) -> MB4 (2) -> MB7 (5)
- Job 6: Factory A MA1 (1) -> MA4 (5) -> MA5 (2) -> MA3 (3) -> MA2 (6) -> MA6 (8) -> MA7 (1); Factory B MB3 (4) -> MB1 (8) -> MB2 (8) -> MB7 (6) -> MB5 (9) -> MB6 (1) -> MB4 (5)

Constraints:
- A machine handles at most one job at a time.
- Each job must respect its listed operation order inside each factory.
- Factory B job Jx cannot start until Factory A job Jx has fully completed.
- When you emit a schedule, every required operation must appear exactly once.

A deterministic feasible baseline schedule is provided below. If you stop immediately or fail to produce a valid schedule, this baseline is what gets scored.
Baseline makespan: 217
Baseline schedule summary:
Factory A: MA1: J1[2-6],J2[55-63],J3[63-65],J4[117-119],J5[122-124],J6[124-125]; MA2: J1[6-15],J2[23-27],J3[85-88],J4[103-108],J5[108-117],J6[135-141]; MA3: J1[15-21],J2[21-23],J3[88-94],J4[94-95],J5[120-122],J6[132-135]; MA4: J1[0-2],J2[46-55],J3[65-69],J4[95-99],J5[124-125],J6[125-130]; MA5: J1[24-33],J2[70-78],J3[78-81],J4[100-103],J5[117-120],J6[130-132]; MA6: J1[33-39],J2[39-46],J3[69-72],J4[99-100],J5[131-134],J6[141-149]; MA7: J1[21-24],J2[63-70],J3[81-85],J4[108-117],J5[125-131],J6[149-150]
Factory B: MB1: J1[56-65],J2[91-99],J3[149-157],J4[157-166],J5[178-180],J6[180-188]; MB2: J1[69-75],J2[107-108],J3[163-168],J4[168-171],J5[171-176],J6[188-196]; MB3: J1[48-56],J2[108-116],J3[140-149],J4[149-150],J5[160-164],J6[164-168]; MB4: J1[67-69],J2[121-124],J3[124-128],J4[128-133],J5[180-182],J6[212-217]; MB5: J1[75-82],J2[82-91],J3[128-131],J4[150-151],J5[151-160],J6[202-211]; MB6: J1[65-67],J2[116-121],J3[131-140],J4[171-172],J5[176-178],J6[211-212]; MB7: J1[39-48],J2[99-107],J3[157-163],J4[172-177],J5[182-187],J6[196-202]
Makespan: 217
